title: Robustly Aerated Croatian Cherry Stew
description: A hearty, peppery stew featuring cherries, made in a cataplana vessel and utilizing an ice-bath shock for a unique texture, this dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner and is vegan-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the cataplana vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground caraway seeds, smoked paprika, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cherries, vegetable broth, and tomato paste, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove the vessel from the heat and immediately submerge it in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the stew cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
Reheat the stew over low heat, seasoning with salt to taste, and serve hot.
